description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a blue crab, specifically the Atlantic blue crab (Callinectes sapidus). Its carapace is broad and slightly convex, colored in shades of greenish-brown with hints of blue, especially on the legs and claws. The claws are large and robust, with the tips and inner edges displaying a striking reddish-orange hue. The legs are slender and jointed, with the last pair flattened into paddle-like shapes, characteristic of swimming crabs. The underside and joints of the limbs show lighter, almost white coloration, contrasting with the darker upper shell. The surface of the shell appears hard and slightly textured, with visible spines and serrations along the edges.
description: A lifelike blue crab with a greenish-brown carapace, blue-tinted legs, and large reddish claws. The shell is hard and textured, with paddle-shaped rear legs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad carapace, both large claws extended, and all legs symmetrically displayed.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the curvature of the shell, one claw raised, and the blue coloration on the legs.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the width of the carapace and the symmetry of the claws and legs.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view with the other claw raised, showing the segmentation and paddle-shaped rear leg.
